name: Papertrail API
what_it_does: Simple cloud log management service with a REST API for searching, tailing, and managing server logs aggregated from syslog, TCP, and HTTP log shipping.
use_cases: Searching recent log events across servers from incident response agents
Creating saved searches and alerts for error pattern monitoring
Managing log source configurations and system registrations
Exporting log archives for long-term storage and compliance
Real-time log tailing via HTTP for debugging workflows
not_for: Large-scale enterprise log analytics (Papertrail is simple by design)
Structured log querying with complex aggregations
Long-term log retention (default retention is 1 year max)
best_when: You need simple, fast log search and aggregation across many servers with minimal configuration — Papertrail is the simplest hosted log management available.
avoid_when: You need complex analytics, structured log parsing, or enterprise SIEM features.

auth.notes: Single API token per account. Token used in X-Papertrail-Token header. Full account access — no fine-grained scopes.

pricing.paid_tiers: Fixa $7/month (1GB/month, 1 week retention); plans scale by log volume
pricing.notes: Straightforward usage-based pricing. SolarWinds-owned. Free tier is genuinely useful for small projects. One of the most affordable hosted log solutions.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Simple JSON errors with message field. Limited error variety — most errors are auth or not-found. API is simple enough that errors are uncommon.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Log search is read-only and idempotent. Saved search creation is not idempotent. Alert creation requires checking for duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: Log search uses simple query syntax (not Elasticsearch DSL) — limited filtering capabilities
Log archive downloads return JSON with log events — must parse each event from the archive format
API does not support writing/ingesting logs — logs are ingested via syslog or HTTP API separately
Search results are capped at 2000 events per request — use cursor pagination for more
Log retention depends on plan tier — queries for logs older than retention return empty results
